specification.topic-statement: The tool management commands allow the user to inspect the active tool registry and reload it from disk, updating the set of tools available to the LLM.

scope.in-scope: The inspect command (listing all tools or showing a single tool's schema) and the refresh command (clearing and rebuilding the registry from tool discovery and internal tool registration).
scope.boundary: How external tool executables are located in the file system and how their schemas are obtained by invoking them is handled by the tool discovery subsystem.
How the registry is structured, how entries are stored and sorted, and how lookups are performed is handled by the tool registry subsystem.
How the per-agent document cache stores and retrieves previously fetched documents is out of scope. The refresh command clears the cache but does not implement it.

behavior.inspect-command:
The inspect command displays information about the tool registry.
When invoked with no argument, all registered tools are listed. For each tool, the name and path are shown. Internal tools show a fixed "(internal)" placeholder in place of a filesystem path. If the registry contains no tools, a message indicating no tools are available is displayed instead of a list.
When invoked with a tool name as its argument, the schema of the named tool is displayed in pretty-printed JSON format, preceded by the tool's name and path. If the named tool is not found in the registry, a message indicating the tool was not found is displayed. A tool not found condition is not treated as an error; the command returns successfully.
All output is appended to the current agent's scrollback.
behavior.refresh-command:
The refresh command rebuilds the tool registry and clears the document cache.
All entries are removed from the tool registry, leaving it empty.
The current agent's document cache is cleared. This ensures that any cached tool schemas are evicted and will be re-fetched on the next access.
Tool discovery is run across the three tool directories (system, user, and project). Each qualifying tool executable is invoked to obtain its schema and is added to the registry.
The built-in internal tools are registered after external discovery completes. An internal tool with the same name as a discovered external tool replaces it.
The registry is sorted alphabetically by tool name after all tools are registered.
A message reporting the total number of tools now in the registry is appended to the current agent's scrollback.
The refresh command reflects changes to tool executables on disk since the last initialization or refresh. New tools become visible, removed tools disappear, and updated schemas take effect.
